primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and low hemoglobin (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection with organ dysfunction. Progressive AKI is evident with staged elevation from creatinine 2.1 mg/dL (Jan 11) to 8.9 mg/dL (Jan 12), though the 8.9 value was excluded as an outlier. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection with acute organ dysfunction
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO stage 1 criteria (1.5× baseline) progressing to stage 3
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline in 7 days

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score ≥1 and approaches severe coagulopathy threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L meets partial criteria, but hypotension and vasopressor requirements not documented
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension with lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order urgent infectious workup including blood cultures and imaging
Start fluid resuscitation and reassess for vasopressor requirements
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Implement hourly urine output monitoring for AKI staging
doctor_handoff: Confirm source of infection, review excluded creatinine outlier for specimen integrity, and assess for new-onset hypotension requiring vasopressors. Prioritize renal function monitoring given AKI progression.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL - excluded as outlier), platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, we've seen signs that your loved one may have a serious infection causing their body to struggle with organ function (sepsis). Their blood tests show high lactate levels, low red blood cells, and a high white blood cell count, which are concerning. Their kidney function has also declined significantly, though one very high kidney test result (creatinine 8.9) is being checked for accuracy. Their platelet count remains low, which affects blood clotting. The care team has started strong antibiotics, is searching for the infection source, and closely monitoring kidney function. They are also preparing for possible fluid support and checking for signs of dangerously low blood pressure. We are carefully reviewing the high creatinine result to confirm if it's accurate.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में हमें लगता है कि आपके प्रिय व्यक्ति में एक गंभीर संक्रमण हो सकता है जो अंगों के काम करने में परेशानी पैदा कर रहा है (सिप्सिस)। उनके ब्लड टेस्ट में लैक्टेट के उच्च स्तर, लाल रक्त कोशिकाओं की कमी और श्वेत रक्त कोशिकाओं की बढ़ोतरी है, जो चिंता का विषय है। उनके गुर्दे का काम भी काफी कमजोर हो गया है, हालांकि एक बहुत ऊँचा गुर्दा टेस्ट परिणाम (क्रिएटिनीन 8.9) की सटीकता की पुष्टि की जा रही है। उनकी प्लेटलेट काउंट अभी भी कम है, जो रक्त के थक्का बनने को प्रभावित करता है। चिकित्सा टीम ने शक्तिशाली एंटीबायोटिक शुरू कर दिए हैं, संक्रमण के स्रोत की जांच कर रहे हैं और गुर्दे के काम की निगरानी कर रहे हैं। वे संभावित तरल समर्थन की तैयारी कर रहे हैं और खतरनाक रूप से कम रक्तचाप के लक्षणों की जांच कर रहे हैं। हम ऊँचे क्रिएटिनीन परिणाम की पुष्टि करने के लिए सावधानी से जांच कर रहे हैं।
